As the Lochra priesthood rose to power in the deep forest, they sent a contingent to the storm temples to learn the secrets of thunder-magic

They returned with a thunderdrake whelp, which in the dim closeness of the forest grew white horns instead of wings, and became the first Blue Wyrm

Ormjarls are masters of Blue Wyrms, though in time many of the beasts would be stolen and raised as mounts for the rapacious Storm Knights, who covet the authority of the towers

Authority over serpents is renowned in the dawn marshes, where monstrous Acroserpents are bent to the will of Keliots

Legend tells of a reptilian wanderer who once passed through the marshes, strangely colored, from the ancient Saurian clan, whose step mesmerized the serpents underfoot

It is said the serpents followed the wanderer out of the marshes in a great slithering mass, and the Snakedancer tradition remains, coveted by the brutal Keliots that rule the whispering reeds

Beastmasters are trainers of rare beast-familiars who once served as arch-priests within the walls of Mardok's stone city

In lost Shuppurak, the Beastmasters gathered for a tournament of might, but were attacked by fire-pledged assassins from the burning lands, who coveted their Brass Dragons

The Beastmasters have since abandoned civilization, wandering the mountains and wild-places as duelists

Raiders of the vast forest, [color=#50af50]green-clad[/color], yelping, a [color=#F0E68C]shimmering horde[/color]

[color=#C71585]Distortion[/color] rules all, O King, stooped to thy [color=#9ACD32]twisting hand[/color]...

It is said the storm temples are the greatest height in Achra; when the world-ending sea rises, the Gothi pledge to return to the blue pillars, to display their glittering art, to become the final beauty of the earth, shining against the plunge of annihilation

In the ancient war that lit the burning lands, it is said a Flame Knight wielded a greatsword forged from a dragon's breath

A follower of Azar, he was executed during the reign of the war-queen

His blazing sword, coveted by a pale, shapeless demon, was lost to the stars

In the burning lands, when the war-queen's reign was broken, a secret coven of sorcerers fled the marble palace, traveling south to the borders of the deep forest

Among the Lochra, they became known as the Topheths, famous for the ritual incineration of their followers, the power to transmute life-force into waves of death; in time, the Tophethic arts were embraced by the skull-wearers of the Lochra priesthood

However, some priests refuse to accept the Topheths, as their history in the burning lands, and true role within the marble palace, has been mysteriously lost
In the distance you hear a shrieking roar

In the gate of death, fortress of the blight-princes, goddess-corpse, where Eresh squats in dim grief, death magic reigns supreme

Those who truly walk the "Path of Death" are therefore said to have gazed "between the teeth of Eresh"

In the expanses of Achra, there are many who carry death's mantle; the ancient, imprisoned cave-god of death, sullen beneath the world; apostate Skin Priests, their crowds of Gehen, with pilgrim Death knights, who seek freedom from the cosmic struggle; wandering Basilisks and Withered Imps, spreading blight-paths; wild Deathdrakes, enraged beneath their decaying curse; skull-wearers of the Lochra priesthood, who seek power through violent rites 

To the sages of the temple-lands, death is the final element, a vitalizing blot in the admixture of the earth

In the hierarchy of the yellow church, those master-servants who choose to venture into Achra are called Mesmers

A Mesmer, seeking the wide lore of the earth, was traveling through lost Shuppurak when rat-men emerged from the plain and assaulted the ruined walls; there was a strange bell-sound, and, it is said, the entire force of rat-men lay down, their minds emptied, yellow-green eyes bulging in the dust

In the mountain temple-lands of Achra, Liturgists are those who lead and combine the element-cults

They are followed by great crowds of zealots, who yearn to one day gather in the sacred pillars, streaked with violet, astral light
In the red depth you sense a gathering of bodies...

In the southern reaches of the mountain temple-lands, the storm temples stand apart, blessed by Hadad himself, it is said, in the first lowering of the waters

It was a sorcerer of the storm temples who called the first thunderdrakes down to Achra

Beasts of sparkling power, their blood was coveted by those who became known as the thunder priests, who abandoned their temples for the hubris of the towers

The "Path of Lightning" is taught by those who remain, who contemplate the storm, how the world may end, a final perfection of wind and light

In the unending battles of Achra, the violence of the path, there are many forms of martial excellence

Champions and duelists roam the coastal plains, teaching the disciplined fighter-lore of lost Shuppurak

In the deep forest, the mountains and wild places, bare-chested wanderers attain brutal might, while assassins perfect their subtle art

The way of "Martial Purity" was taught by a nameless Strijela of the eastern desert; in her words, "Nothing is greater in life; all are welcome to the contest of force"

It is said evil vines grow here

It is said fire was once the domain of Phoenix, a star-goddess of Achra, before it was stolen by Azar, who carried it to the lands below to revive Agni, his divine twin

In the burning lands, the last followers of Azar were slaughtered by excorites of the war-queen upon her violent ascension, while priests of Agni fled into the wilderness

Beyond, to the many cults of Achra, fire magic is a symbol of both destruction and vitality

The "Path of Fire" is taught by flame priests, who worship in the mountain temple-lands, among pillars of orange stone
It is said pale warriors lurk here

It is said that Achra was once a world of cities, strung with wide-stone paths, until the coming of plague, the destroyer of delights

Plaguemancers, in somber covens, keep dim remembrance of this devastation

Among the Brud of the filth-valleys, Plaguemancers are well-known as prophets and terrible, nation-destoying mystics, reaching far beyond the natural indignities of death

It is said that the northern filth-valleys were disgorged by the green mother, after the first lowering of the waters, in disgust at the sudden brilliance of the stars

In that place of wild sickness, the Uspori are those who craft a unique paralyzing poison, through a secret ritual performed in hidden caves

Accompanied by monstrous Bukavac, the Uspori are feared skirmishers, and often stand against blight-raiders from the gate of death
It is said the "hungry god" lives here
It is said this place is haunted by a "Lord of Mouths"

It was a worn-down [color=#FF4500]chariot land[/color]. A land of [color=#7FFFD4]glittering cities[/color] and horned phalanxes bleeding their [color=#ffa000]glory[/color] across the rippling plains

This was the shaping of [color=#ffff50]Achra[/color], O King. Changes witnessed by the [color=#CD5C5C]changeless depth[/color]. My [color=#FFDAB9]ash-birth[/color]. This I know

In [color=#FF00FF]legend[/color] the universe is set. The gaze of [color=#7CFC00]divinity[/color] yearns for [color=#ff0000]one alone[/color], to fill it. To cast thy [color=#DA70D6]shadow[/color] in every corner. O King, to [color=#FFA07A]consume[/color] all horizons...

It was an Elementalist who conjured the first temple of Achra, fabricating it from the young mountains and dedicating it to Spectra, the vanished goddess of rainbows, birth and the once-living sea. The temple is now considered nameless and lost, buried by the new deities of the temple-lands or, according to certain sages of the wilderness, hidden under the towers of the path

In the distortion of the ages, it has become increasingly rare for a true Elementalist to rise from the kinetic arts, though aspirant cultists often claim this title

It was an Inuqqa of the Arjana who first stood against the Surtmir onslaught, as they poured blue-furred and monstrous from their volcano

The Inuqqa entered a trance; in a pillar of cold blue light, death rained down on the invaders

Though a Surtmir war-king finally trumphed, beheading the Inuqqa, a great mound of his comrades lay withered and slain upon the frozen waste

Long ago, a priest of lost Shuppurak made pilgrimage to the dawn marshes, and learned of a marsh-cleric who could take on serpent-shape

Coveting this power, the priest stole the scroll of changing, but was instead cursed by the cleric, warped into a monstrous, chimeric form; he became the first Shapeshifter, whose green-hued blood craves transformation

Many who walk the path of Achra crave the great glory-won powers of magic and skill

Rare pilgrims will abstain from the great powers, keeping a vow of humility

To ones such as these, glory alone is the prize, a divine mystery that glimmers above the earth
Masked regent sent to the gate of horn. Exiled long ago from a broken tower. Awaits the 'wandering king' of the star-path. Presides over monstrosities of thought

Most Dreamers serve the goddess Mehtar, who slumbers deep within the high glaciers, guarding the world's edge with phantasms of ice

Some, however, abstain from Mehtar's gift, choosing instead to wander Achra

It is said a Dreamer once came to the yellow church, to challenge the psychic art of the master-servants

A battle ensued, a great spiral of blue light, it is said, and in the fading dusk a gentle rain fell on the salt waste

Necromancers are those committed to the lore of reanimation, of death-from-life

Desiring power, or striving for their own ends, they often stand opposed to edicts passed by the gate of death, and for generations have been hunted by its blight-princes

O King, here these [color=#6495ED]truths[/color], that no account may surpass

The [color=#4169E1]ocean[/color] swells: annihilation. [color=#a0a000]Death[/color] lumbers underground, sullen in his [color=#BC8F8F]cave[/color] of dry stone. The realm of [color=#F4A460]dust[/color] is boundless, it is beyond your concern, it is beyond your [color=#7FFFD4]iteration[/color]

My folk crusade now among the [color=#EE82EE]dazzling stars[/color], yearning for the conquest of light. Your power, [color=#FFFF00]radiance[/color], that demolishes all [color=#B22222]constraint[/color]...

O King, see [color=#ffff50]Achra[/color], in the first [color=#DA70D6]unraveling[/color] of light

Continent of [color=#DC143C]monster-gods[/color]. A roaring [color=#8FBC8F]wilderness[/color] of vines, ruled by deathless folk of the [color=#00FF7F]green passages[/color], of limitless contempt. Dreadful [color=#20af60]Banrion[/color]...

So it is said! History is the quickening of [color=#ff2020]blood[/color], the [color=#CD853F]collapse[/color] of blood, a vast and vile [color=#6A5ACD]exhaustion[/color]

May your [color=#00FFFF]fist[/color] close on it all, may it be the [color=#8A2BE2]final closing[/color], O King of [color=#DDA0DD]broken light[/color]...

Driven by revenge or sheer tenacity, they are often hunted to brutal annihilation, erased from the memory of the earth, but for those washed in the utmost purity of violence, striding glorious through the howling wastelands of the path

Strange red blooms cling to the walls. You smell ash

Terrified travellers speak of the "golden king"

The "Path of Life," all magic of growth, springs from the first grove, divine forest of the ancient earth

Pride of the green mother, her children dwelled there in splendor until it was destroyed by a warrior-king, who, seeking glory, felled the giant life-giving pines with a legendary cleaver

This, it is said, is how the divine siblings Ninhurs and Humbaba, filled with poison and rage, became king-haters, enemies of the Obelisk until the last rising of the waters

Ferods and other monsters, vengeance-blind fragments of the grove's magic, now wander the deep forest of the West, while the hidden nation of Briarfolk prays for its final, apocalyptic return

The "lawless beast" from underground; presides over hunger, nameless chasms and monstrosity. Loathes the stars and demands the blood-offering of one's followers, seeking the divine upheaval of the earth
The "poisoned stone" of lost Shuppurak; forbidden Goddess of jade. Rewards slowness

The Acolytes were once a sect of the Alhaja, who seek the highest piety

Now, all Acolytes gather in a sacred yurt-city, in the desert of the gods, where they fast and burn incense, kneeling beneath plumes of dark-violet smoke

It is said their minds are filled with death and sacrifice, pleasing to divinity, which soars above every indulgence

The Alizeh arose from a sect within the storm temples, Tengu priests of Auzom, the star-wind, child of Agara

Though Auzom's name was lost, the Alizeh tradition persevered, and in time was brought to the Qamar of the astral wall, who welcomed the cursed bird-forms of the Tengu

The Alizeh now serve as rangers of the void, dancing swiftly along paths of blue-violet light

The Angioks are blessed priests of the Inuga, sworn enemies of the monstrous Tupilaq

By Inuga creed, the title of Angiok is available to all wanderers, provided they submit to the ordeal of spirit, the sacred emptying of heat

The outskirts of Inuga villages often contain the frozen bodies of failed Angioks, crouched like shrines, lost to the swirling earth

The Aqliyya are a rebel sect of the yellow church 

Devoted to mind-fighting, they wander the salt waste and the desolate western mountains as sages and bringers of justice, often battling fanatic Brungle clans that seek to raid Achra from their worm-pits

The Arch-Lizards are a sect of "pure" shapeshifters who trace their origin to the lost temples of the dawn marshes

Beneath the mountains, where an underground river fans out into the coastal plains, the marshes were once ruled by serpent-kings in noble splendor

Now it is a place of brutality, the endless hunting-ground of keliots and their servant-beasts

The Arsonists began as an apostate-sect of grove priests who preached the ritual burning of life, to make room for fresh growth

When the forests of the West were threatened by a rampaging firedrake, a coven of Arsonists lured the beast into the mountains, where they snuffed out its fire with charred, surging roots, summoned from their fervent prayers

Arsonists are now honored allies of the grove cult, restoring the forests of Achra with invigorating heat

The Berserkers are brutal fighters who roam the cold foothills of the deep forest

In combat, they mar themselves with ice, blood and deathly curses, known as the "three kings of violence"

Berserkers are held sacred by the Lochra priesthood, where they are anointed as living offerings to the triumphant mysteries of pain

The Bheith Nochti are sacred bare-chested fighters that once roamed the desert of the gods

In the purity of the desert, it is said, they found a secret place of absolute piety; in the hissing sand, a smooth terrace of white otherworldly stone

The Blood Mages learned their magic from Irga festivals, deep in the tunnel-lands, in the chasms above the infernal plane

They call upon the "inner blood," a source of power, hidden in the surpassing dark

All skin, all flesh, they say, is an overfull jar, a thing to be opened

The Blood-Drinkers began as a sect of Morlocks, devoting their bodies to sacred life-thirst

Legends still remain of their emergence, where in one howling night they glutted themselves on the vitrea of cave-folk, in the hills below the Naqui temples

Though the sect was annihilated by a Naqui Flame Knight, their cursed, invigorating desire entered the magic of Achra, waiting for those who choose to grasp it, deep in the pull of blood

The Damunja are assassins who trace their art to a wandering night-serpent that escaped a red-hued vortex in the stars

When the serpent came to Achra, it taught the secrets of blood to the Damunja, along with the horror of the thunder-curse, which became their mark

In time, the serpent lost its power of speech and flight, and withdrew into the tunnel-lands, where it was sacrificed in an Irga festival of blood

It is said a red mist still presides in the deep chasms of the earth

The Death Knights were once Skeleton-thralls of a blight-prince, waging war for the gate of death

In a far-ranging raid through Achra, the Death Knights came upon the desert of the gods, where it is said Ashem smiled, and restored their will

The Death Knights rebelled, overwhelming their prince with heavy, relentless blows, until his corpse was finally abandoned to the whirling sands

The Dianmai rose to prominence in the bouts of lost Shuppurak, where they learned to sicken their foes with a secret bare-fisted technique

They now wander Achra as duelists and assassins, though many still worship Yu, the "poison stone" of that doomed city; these Dianmai make pilgrimage to the tunnel-lands beneath the dawn marshes, in search of the legendary jade polis of Yu, that holds countless blessings and relic-prisms of subtle green light

The Frenzied Ones trace their cult to a prince-consort of the burning lands, once beloved of the war-queen

In the depths of the marble palace, the prince discovered a red mask, a relic of Phoenix, that inflamed his spirit past all constraint

So masked, the prince hefted his golden axe and abandoned the war-queen to wage war upon the towers of Achra, screaming, it is said, of a great path in the stars

The Gallic order was born from the death of the Bull-Giant

Desiring the power of earth-shaking, the first Gallus wore armor crafted from the giant's dense bones
The God of chaos and disembodiment. Scorned father of the gruesome Umbraliths. Rewards constant teleportation
The God of discipline and cosmic sanctity. Worshipped as a divine judge by those who wander the desert of the gods. Rewards correct ritual procedure

The God of plagues, whose visage is a jeweled altar of dark stone, hidden in the eastern desert. Known as the "shadow that follows the wind," Takhal is the eternal enemy of Tengri, and is said to grant a wretched vitality
The God of sorcerers; lord of the four calamities. Patriarch of the divine siblings Ragna, Auzom, Sita and Rabal. Empowers followers with destructive magic
The God of stoic resolve and honorable combat. Dwells within a nameless mountain in the wilderness of Achra. Rewards injury and perseverance
The God of the cosmic anthill whose children still dwell in the fading tunnels of the astral dark. Rewards those who summon and fight alongside his holy ants
The God of the cutting wind. Inhabits the open sky of the eastern desert. Rewards alacrity and risk
The God of the golden tomb. The slain consort of Eris. Rewards the preservation of his sacred entourage and the accumulation of armor
The God of the lost stone city of beasts. Blesses followers with a heavy-horned "Helm of Mardok"
The God of wildfire and storms. Presided over the first lowering of the waters. Favors those who perform the dance of pure combat
The Goddess of defense and craftiness. Was once the divine guardian of lost Shuppurak. Rewards Off-hand combat and blocking
The Goddess of growth and decay. Vigorous offspring of the green mother. Rewards the sacrifice of enemies upon her divine grass
The Goddess of ice and illusions. Said to dwell within the high glaciers at the edge of the world. Rewards descent into her immaculate dream
The Goddess of life-hunger and the wretched dead. Presides in grief within the corpse of her divine mother. Exults in the death of allies and the gathering of artifacts
The Goddess of struggle. Worshipped by the divine warrior Dumuzi before his ascent to godhood. Rewards individual power
The Goddess of the lost sun. The vanished "first light" of Achra. Allows worshippers to rebirth themselves in glorious flames

The Ice Shahs are an Arba-founded cult of the northern waste, who perfect their magic of animate-ice at the foot of the high glaciers

All around their hidden temple, it is said, spreads an enchanted tundra of cold, green-blue vines, that guards them against the bronze-masked Granga, exiles of the blue tower, who pillage from their dwelling-palaces deep in the crevasse-lands

The Inuga Frost Knights of the high glaciers once stood against the Surtmir armies, repelling them to the crevasse-lands below with their chilling shield-magic

The tradition was preserved as a glorification of ice, the end of depredation

The Kashra are valiant star-warriors that sojourn beyond the astral wall to battle the champions of the Pentot, purple-masked demons of the astral dark

Though the Pentot are numerous and powerful, they refuse to invade Achra so long as a Kashra lives, preferring the duel-festival, the honored contest of power, glorious in the shimmering arches of night

The Mind Knights are noble warriors of the yellow church, trained by the master-servants to channel their willpower into martial skill

When heliots surrounded the church and threatened its ruin, a Mind Knight challenged the arch-heliot to a ritualistic duel

The white sun glinted on her arcing blade, it is said, and the heliots fled in terror as their leader fell, immaculately sliced in half upon the salt waste

The Night-Crows began as a sect of sorcerers who were cast out from the gate of death

Dressed in humiliating feathered garments, they wandered the bleak northern mountains until they came upon the corpse of a giant, iridescent crow; the sorcerers ate of its magic, decaying flesh, forever gaining its power and shape

The Night-Crows are now called the "wild path" for aspiring mystics, who wish to pursue the glory of the stars

The Paragons were founded by the first, ancient king of lost Shuppurak

It is said the Paragon-anointed army of those days was unconquerable, turquoise-armored, bristling with immaculate lances; they famously slayed a Cave Worm of the deep earth, in a battle that flattened the coastal plains

The Path of Achra, island of towers, is said to be at the spiritual center

Those who rule the towers claim dominion over Achra

When the Obelisk rose, last from the dune sea, history became another distortion of light

The astral wall was pierced

Beyond the star-path, against the surpassing dark, a final dominion was claimed

So began the great night of Achra, of the roiling, world-ending sea

Here, pilgrims seek their glory, as a terrible wind howls, at the edges of all things...

The Sahasi are sacred warriors of the mountain temple-lands, guardians of the artless initiates and lore-seekers, who crave the succor of mystery

When the temple-stones were young, there was a prophetic Ur-Magus who spoke of a great war annihilating the stars; the Sahasi order was formed around this secret fear

As they grow in strength, Sahasi often make pilgrimage to the astral wall, where they pledge to defend Achra against unknown horrors of the glimmering dark

The Shazuza are disciples of a forgotten calamity, the "sickening thunder" that once swept the Eastern desert

The first Shazuza was a sublime Ihra, who, defying the limits of flesh, learned to beckon lightning to smite the poisoned blood of her enemies

She became a terrible destroyer of rat-men, who, in those days, still swarmed up through the festering, many-tunneled dark

The Suwag are wandering fighters who enchant their weapons with animate gore

It is said the first Suwag was an Albaz warrior who explored deep into the tunnel-lands where she discovered a secret church of blood; hoping to purify the weaknesses of her blade, she embraced the church's painful, transforming mysteries

The Uhratas of Achra are master prayer-singers, consigning their bodies to divine judgement

It was a sect of ecstatic Uhratas who first made pilgrimage to the shores of the path, rowing magnificent ribbon-hung ships from the ruined cove of lost Shuppurak; it is said their pious song was the first offering to the shadows of the towers

The Ur-Beast is a spirit of the deep forest, whose aspect is often invoked by battle-crazed fighters of the berserkergang

Within the Lochra priesthood, the Ur-Beast's intense fury is viewed as both a miracle and a deep wretchedness, a violence that both preserves and consumes the world

It is the fate of many who invoke the Ur-Beast to finally degrade into Gora, yelping hounds of mindless bloodlust, banished to the astral dark, howling ever-hungry above the lands of Achra

The Vengati are a sect of Mesmers from the yellow church who took up residence in lost Shuppurak, where they were inspired by Yu, a goddess contained in a jade sculpture, known as the "poisoned stone"

Through the proliferation of sickness, the Vengati amplified their psychic art, though it is often said these practices sped the downfall of that great city

The Venites are fighters trained in the way of poison, the "shimmering middle" that overcomes both life and death

Though they often defend the northern filth-valleys against blight priests and wandering monsters from the frozen waste, many Venites travel the lands of Achra, teaching their discipline

Dancing on the edge of decay, remembering the green mother, who died long ago, they sing "O cusp! O shimmering emerald cusp!"

The Worm-Born follow in the ways of an exiled grove priest

Upon facing starvation in the deep forest of the West, the priest performed a desperate life-beckoning ritual

His body was fused with a multitude of emerald-hued worms, the swarming corpse-eaters of that wild place

The air distorts in shimmering curls

The crystalline warren, secret burrow-temple of sorcerers and knights, who covet their inward-facing meditative arts, sometimes dispatches pilgrims to the surface lands of Achra

These are known as the Mura, mirror-summoners, who by arcane edict seek to reflect the world back upon itself, to redeem the warren's unknown mission beneath the earth
The culture of not being alive: has many powerful [color=#ffff00]resistances[/color]
The destructive animus of a Torch Priest
The divine eye that once stood at the end of the star-path. Now sealed in glimmering stone. Summons enchanted Prismas of the three sacred hues
The eternal Worm that slumbers beneath the desert of the gods. Provides a mighty aura of psychic corrosion through the Du' Eird ritual of mental sacrifice

The first Gliva was a wandering druid who, during a pilgrimage into the tunnel-lands, discovered the "hidden song" of the mushrooms, a powerful psychic communion

Seeking to spread their faith, Glivas now wander the deep forest of the West, where they are persecuted by the Lochra priesthood, who above all worship the redemptive possibilities of violence

The Glivas claim that one day the mushrooms will overtake the surface of Achra, and the first grove of the child-gods will be reborn in an image of fungal splendor

The first Mindhunter was an initiate of the yellow church who questioned the wisdom of the master-servants, fearing the intoxication of psychic power

Exiled to the salt waste, he was abducted by Heliots and subjected to their terrible rites of dissolution

Against this suffering, in the furious revolt of his spirit, it is said, the exile gained the secrets of blood-magic, and thereby became a deadly assassin, the first Mindhunter, eternal enemy and brutal mirror of the disciplined Mind Knights of the yellow church

The first Pyromaniac was a flame priest of the mountain temple-lands who discovered the secret syllable of fire, and was bestowed with an endless "heightening"

Pyromaniacs are those who seek this hidden sound, inflaming themelves through ritual acts of incineration; it was a Pyromaniac who brazenly summoned the first untamed Red Dragon from within the sacred mountain

He was devoured, it is said, and the beast, brimming with swallowed magic, rampaged into the world-ending sea

The first Samnites were bare-chested fighters of the deep forest, eaters of a magic vine

The strongest among them journeyed to the great bouts of lost Shuppurak, where they secured renown, as few could stand against their irrepressible vim

The first Shantih was a Kull orphan raised in the storm temples of Achra

A master of the "lightning-dance," she became a legendary duelist that wandered the coastal plains, gathering followers

Her death came at the hands of an unnatural, voiceless knight, clad in violet armor, fallen from a path in the stars

The folk of Achra who pass into the stars are said to feel a joyous emancipation

To the Qamar of the astral wall, this freedom is cautioned as a spiritual ailment, a fall to edgelessness

The most ailed, the most joyful, are called Starjumpers, of those who wander the violet paths, who leap the widening dark

The glittering tunnel fades into a turquoise dark...

You are not alone...
The ground is littered with discarded scales
The ground is marred by coiling trails of blight
The heights of the tower seem dissolved. You smell melting stone
The light seems to freeze into strange emerging shapes

The long serpent of the outer dark, the dust-void past all light. Apophis is the ancient enemy of Phoenix. Those who worship Apophis invite their own destruction, in exchange for unnatural, ever-increasing Strength
The moistened edge of the astral wall was once the vital 'estuary' between starlight and flesh. The Waking Flower now claims supremacy; by its perfumed fruits a glimmering dominion is spread...
The monster-Goddess of the devouring night. A sibling of the "star-eaters" who was long ago cast down to Achra. Rewards those who wear her corrupt vestiges
The muck is rippled by writhing serpentine shapes
The path is lined with strangled half-digested corpses
The rat-men have raised their flag here
The rocks are smeared with dung. A terrible stench

The stone Goddess of draconic transformation whose body dwells deep within the mountain temple-lands. Blesses followers with a fearsome Drakeform
The stones bear strange yellow markings
The tower is encrusted with unnatural whorls of ice

The way of "Psychic Purity" is embodied by master-servants of the yellow church

The church contains powerful relics of the ancient earth, and is defended by a militant priesthood against mindhunters and pale heliots of the salt waste

Many of these priests, seduced by the boundlessness of their ability, are themselves driven into exile

By "dreaming beyond the stars," it is said, they sojourn through the gate of horn, to the terrible mind-warren of the astral dark, where they form a pact with a gold demon by drinking its ichor

These intoxicated priests, still adorned in the bright robes of the yellow church, seek to cross the astral wall and discover the "room at the end of the mind," where a great truth awaits them, shut past all thought
The wilderness-born God of war and bloodshed. Despises civilization. Rewards the amplification of rage
There are antler-sigils sketched in the dust

There are lustrous banners bearing a golden sigil
There are sudden flashes of blue light

There are vast lands of Achra where ice rules all; the frozen waste, the ice caves of the Albaz, the crevasse-lands

Greatest are the high glaciers, world-spanning, final edge of the divine gaze, border of chaos, birthplace of the Siku, where none have passed

Those who seek the "Path of Ice," Angioks, priests, often suffer its intoxication, and in a frenzy make war upon the middle lands of Achra, as annihilators of decay, heat, formlessness itself

According to Inuga sages, this frenzy is what drove the Surtmir army to its final, gruesome invasion of the infernal plane

There are whole skeletons cleaned of flesh

There is a crumbling vine-wrapped altar

There is a great mound of skulls covered in moss and shimmering purple flowers
There is a rumor of children vanishing
There is a rumor of marauders adorned in gold
There is an unusual silence. A strange light flickers in the distance

There was once a nameless Stran of the Eastern desert, whose companion animal was slain by a Torch Priest

The Stran descended on the tents of the torch-coven, her furious javelins flying in every direction; an Amir found her kneeling, grief-stricken among the impaled priests

The name he granted her, Strijela, has since become a title of great renown

Those who poison their own blood to "seek abomination" are called Merzot

When the Surtmirs sought to invade the filth-valleys, it was the Merzot who drove them back, with waves of foul-smelling gore

Those who pursue meditation past the body's own life-limits, it is said, receive a new form, that draws from the root of life, the first grove whose worldly presence was destroyed long ago

Practitioners take on the shape of an ancient, divine star-tree, called the tree of Arborus, glittering with flocks of cosmic wisps, hatched from the pink stars of the night

Though cults of poison thrive in the deep forest and dawn marshes, nowhere does its magic hold greater sway than the northern filth-valleys

All poison comes from the green mother, it is said, the source of all life, who the Brud of the filth-valleys once worshipped during the reign of the oozes

In the aging of Achra, the green mother became "divided from life" and lay down in the mountains

A dark-violet citadel rose from her corpse, now called the gate of death, throne of the blight-princes

Though the Brud have turned to doom-magic, the green mother is still invoked by Venites, great fighters who spread the "Path of Poison" throughout Achra, the "shimmering middle" between life and death

Though the "Path of Blood" is widely preached by gore cults who roam the mountains and deserts of Achra, blood-magic is traced to stranger realms

Deep in the tunnel-lands, above the infernal plane, the red-furred Irga congregate in festivals of blood, where they sacrifice groaning beasts to the "inner blood" that "surges beyond the dark"

In the temple-lands, Blood Mages often speak of this place; a gyre of blood rotating through the stars, itself a great red star, curled like a snake, that presides within the blood-pull of life, a font to which all history vanishes

To be Surtmir once meant to pursue the sublime mixture of fire and ice, as taught by Ragna, the dead god of the frozen waste

After a power struggle, many of the Surtmir clans, intoxicated by ice magic, became monstrous warrior-giants, forming a terrible army

Those who keep the old ways still invoke the ancient heart of Ragna, a ruddy ember that trembles beneath the shining ice

Torturers, it is said, first learned their art of the "sacred agony" from an exiled Slagh priest, who fled the deep forest for the safety of the winding mountains

Though Torturers are often shunned in the temple-lands, they remain unmatched in subduing the giant Obars that rampage from the tunnels, who obey nothing but pain
Travellers claim the "nameless blood" once ruled here
Travellers claim the "tangled worm" once ruled here
Travellers claim the "will of Agni" once ruled here
Travellers speak of the "wings of death"

Weird bolts of violet lightning pulse overhead

When a Qamar sojourner came to the yellow church, she founded the cult of the Psychonauts, a hidden path by which priests could enter the stars

This path is studied in secret, for adept Psychonauts rarely return to Achra, preferring to flit through the passages of cosmic light, fearing the encumbrance of the earth

When a storm-monk made pilgrimage to the yellow church, to learn from the master-servants there, she founded Samsi, the way of brilliance

For many years, her "yellow lightning" would persist as an overwhelming weapon against mindhunters and heliots of the salt waste

When a wandering priest of Agni took refuge in the storm temples, he abandoned the destroyer-god and began worshipping Rabal, a young storm-god of cataclysm

Rabal grants power to all who perform his ritual of immolation; those who pursued this dangerous art became known as Tamasas

The priest and his Tamasa followers were soon exiled from the storm temples for their recklesness as taboo-violators, for the profane act of self-incineration

When a wide-ranging Arjana came to the storm temples of Achra, on a trade journey south, he was initiated into the blue-pillared hall, and learned the ways of Hadad, the storm-god at the beginning of the world

He became the first Stormbringer, famous among his folk; countless Granga, crawling up from the crevasse-lands, seeking to pillage Arjana wealth, were annihilated by his magic gale

When an Albaz traveler made pilgrimage to the storm temples, passing first through the gate of death, she was outcast for bearing the touch of blight

In the ravine-lands far below the temples, she founded the dreaded cult of Bolga, the unending black-clouded storm that revels in death

When an exiled heliot of the salt waste came to the temples of the Naqui, she was welcomed into their culture of attunement, and became a renowned sage

In time, she synthesized the fire magic of the temple-lands with her own rites of corrosion, and so arose the first Hazars, masters of the acid-ground, who patrol the western mountains, defending against monsters of the deep forest

When hobgoblins, coveting the secrets of gold, made war upon the mountain temple-lands, it was a lone Arch-Magus who defended the pillared gate

It is said the Arch-Magus sank into a deep trance; within moments, the hobgoblins were destroyed in a hiss of astral flame, reduced to shapeless lights, that swayed like a field of transparent violet flowers

When the divine gaze first rained light through the void, the stars lay upon Achra as glittering forests

In the blooming of the dark, the gaze recoiled from its own radiance; all was drawn upward, the stars like beads on a string

Thus, the star-path was formed, as written in the tract of Ikshana

Accordingly, those who seek "Astral Purity" yearn continually upward, from the wilds of Achra to the temple-lands, to the towers of the path and the astral wall, that guards against beasts of the star-wind, and beyond...

Aspirant archi-magi, questing knights of the crystalline warren, Psychonauts, the "wandering king" himself, all have pursued their art toward the legendary star-path, often to their doom, where Pentots, brutal violet-armored knights and great demons of nameless stature roam the passages of light

When the green mother lived, the filth-valleys were a land of oozes, shimmering emerald creatures of elemental poison

In time, those who made pacts with the oozes became Oozemancers, and took up rulership of the filth-valleys 

With the rise of the gate of death, the power of the oozes faded, though rare Oozemancers still wander the dim, poison mists

When the green mother perished, Brud legend speaks of  an "ill star" that appeared in the sky

Ill Prophets are disciples of this star, and are said to themselves emit a strange light that warps the land itself into a glimmering, poisonous waste

Above the filth-valleys, there is a purple-hued mountain, whose slopes were long ago lost to the work of the Prophets, who prepare for the day when their star falls to Achra, and the spirit of the green mother is revived

Windblades once roamed the Eastern desert as fighter-mystics, lending their skill to warring Amirs

In their tradition, it is said, "No law, no heart's distemper, surpasses the nobility of thy flashing blade"

You see the dim outline of florid dancers
You see the dissolved remnants of armor
You see the remains of a crude altar. You smell putrefying flesh
You see the remains of a defiled shrine
You see the remnants of shattered weapons. You smell blood
You see trails of quivering fungus
You see wreaths of coiling chitin
You sense a terrible crushing blankness
You smell an alarming insectoid musk
You smell smoke. The ground trembles
You smell the reek of sulfur. There are red cracks in the dark
You smell the stench of lightning

You walk into an expanse of dunes and ridged stone...

Something is coming...

You worship [color=#20af20]Ninhurs, the Green Mother[/color]. 

As a new follower, you may call upon [color=#20af20]Ninhurs[/color] to heal you and cleanse you of [color=#70ff00]Sickness[/color] through recitation of the [color=#20af20]Leget Mhor[/color] [color=#ffff50]I[/color]. 

As your glory increases, [color=#20af20]Ninhurs[/color] will grant you the ability to deal high [color=#70ff00]Poison[/color] damage to nearby enemies through the [color=#20af20]Uttuk Alu[/color] [color=#ffff50]II[/color] and to summon divine serpents through the [color=#20af20]Loh Musma[/color] [color=#ffff50]III[/color].

[color=#20af20]Ninhurs[/color] rewards your presence on [color=#ffff50]divine grass[/color] with increased healing and the application of [color=#60af20]Entangle[/color] on enemies. As enemies die on [color=#ffff50]divine grass[/color], the [color=#20af20]Leget Mhor[/color] and [color=#20af20]Uttuk Alu[/color] will rapidly regain charges.

You worship [color=#8080f0]Ashem, the Righteous[/color]. 

As a new follower, you may call upon [color=#8080f0]Ashem[/color] to restore your life through recitation of the [color=#8080f0]Med Vohu[/color] [color=#ffff50]I[/color]. 

As your glory increases, [color=#8080f0]Ashem[/color] will grant you the ability to channel your attributes into [color=#8030af]Astral[/color] damage through the [color=#8080f0]Yazata[/color] [color=#ffff50]II[/color] and [color=#8080f0]Vendi[/color] [color=#ffff50]III[/color]. 

[color=#8080f0]Ashem[/color] admires correct ritual procedure; each prayer is charged by the preceding prayer, and all prayers grant temporary [color=#80a0f0]Grace[/color], which reduces the damage you take

You worship [color=#a03030]Humbaba, the Beast of War[/color]. 

As a new follower, you may call upon [color=#a03030]Humbaba[/color] to grant [color=#a03030]Bloodrage[/color] through recitation of the [color=#a03030]Sum Uri[/color] [color=#ffff50]I[/color]. [color=#a03030]Humbaba[/color] rewards several applications of [color=#a03030]Bloodrage[/color], granting increased healing when you hit enemies and multiplying the effects of [color=#a03030]Ur Halhala[/color].
